AnalyzeCommand_Description: Runs code analyzers on the current project or on a list of source files.
AnalyzeCommand_Remarks:
A code analyzer is a tool that examines source code for potential issues and style violations. Code analyzers other than the default one are installed as part of compiler extensions (packages). Managing compiler extensions is facilitated through the 'dc package' command.

The '--markers' option provides a simple way to scan for code comments with marker symbols such as 'TODO', 'NOTE' or 'FIXME'. It searches through the current project or specified files and displays all according comments in a structured list.
AnalyzeCommand_AnalyzerOptionDescription: The name of the code analyzer to run. If none is specified, the default analyzer is used.
AnalyzeCommand_FilesOptionDescription: A list of source files to analyze. If this option is not used, all source files in the current project will be analyzed.
AnalyzeCommand_MarkersOptionDescription: Extracts and displays all comments containing markers such as TODO from the current project or the specified source files.
AnalyzeCommand_MarkerOptionDescription: Specifies a custom marker to include in the search. Multiple can be specified.
AnalyzeCommand_ExcludeOptionDescription: Specifies a marker to ignore in the search. Multiple can be specified.
AnalyzeCommand_Example1: Runs the default code analyzer on all source files in the current project.
AnalyzeCommand_Example2: Runs 'CustomAnalyzer' on all source files in the current project.
AnalyzeCommand_Example3: Runs the default code analyzer on the specified source files.
AnalyzeCommand_Example4: Runs 'CustomAnalyzer' on the specified source files.
AnalyzeCommand_Example5: Displays all comments with markers in the current project.
AnalyzeCommand_SourceFileNotFound: The file '{0}' could not be found.
AnalyzeCommand_AnalyzerNotFound: The analyzer '{0}' could not be found.
AnalyzeCommand_NoProjectFileFound: The current directory contains no project file.
AnalyzeCommand_FailedToCollectFiles: Could not determine source files to analyze: {0}
BuildCommand_Description: Executes the specified build profile, or compiles all source files in the current folder structure if none is specified.
BuildCommand_Remarks: This is the primary command for building Dassie projects. By default, this command will compile all Dassie source files in the current directory as well as all subdirectories. If no project file is present in the root directory, the default configuration is used.
BuildCommand_BuildProfileOptionDescripti: Specifies the build profile to execute. If not set, the default profile is executed.
BuildCommand_OptionsOptionDescription: Additional options to pass to the compiler. For a list of available options, use 'dc help -o'.
BuildCommand_Example1: Builds the current project with the default build profile.
BuildCommand_Example2: Builds the current project with the 'CustomProfile' build profile.
BuildCommand_Example3: Builds the current project with the 'CustomProfile' build profile using the AOT compiler.
BuildCommand_ProjectGroupNotSupported: 'dc build' can only be called on single projects. Use 'dc deploy' to build and deploy a project group.
BuildCommand_BuildProfileNotFound: The build profile '{0}' could not be found.
BuildCommand_FailedToCollectFiles: Files to compile could not be determined: {0}
CleanCommand_Description: Clears build artifacts and temporary files of a project or project group.
CleanCommand_Remarks: This command only works when executed at the root level of a project or project group. It deletes all output and temporary files generated by the compiler. Warning: This command will delete all contents of the build directory, even those created by the user!
CleanCommand_Example: Cleans the build artifacts and temporary files of the current project or project group.

CompileCommand_AdvancedOptionsLine2: For simple properties of type 'string', 'bool' or 'enum'. The property name is case-insensitive. For boolean properties, 0 and 1 are aliases for false and true. Example: --MeasureElapsedTime=true
CompileCommand_AdvancedOptionsLine3: To add elements to an array property. Property names are recognized by the first characters, where 'References' takes precedence over 'Resources'. Example: --R+"assembly.dll"
CompileCommand_AdvancedOptionsLine4: For setting child properties of more complex objects. Object names are recognized by first characters. Example: --VersionInfo::Description="Application"

ConfigCommand_Description: Manages compiler settings and project configurations.
ConfigCommand_PropertyOption: The property to modify. Multiple can be specified, separated by spaces. The value is optional, if omitted, the default value is used. Note that the equals sign (=) is still required.
ConfigCommand_GlobalOption: Indicates that the operation displays or modifies the global configuration, as opposed to a project file.
ConfigCommand_ResetOption: Resets all global properties to their default value.
ConfigCommand_ImportOption: Imports the global configuration from the specified file.
ConfigCommand_Remarks:
This command is used to display or change global or project-specific compiler settings. If this command is called without arguments in a directory containing a project file, it will display the current project configuration. Similarly, the '--global' flag is used to change or show the global configuration.

If 'dc config' is called in a directory not containing a project file, a new project file will be created. This is useful for initializing a Dassie project in an existing directory structure, as opposed to the 'dc new' command which creates a new project structure based on a template. If property values are supplied to the command as arguments, they will be applied to the generated project file.
ConfigCommand_Example1: Creates a new project file with default values in the current directory, or displays the current project configuration if one already exists.
ConfigCommand_Example2: Changes two settings of the project configuration.
ConfigCommand_Example4: Changes a setting of the global configuration.
ConfigCommand_ImportFilePathRequired: File path to import from required.

DbgCommand_Description: Commands used for debugging and testing the compiler. To be used by compiler developers.

DeployCommand_Description: Builds and deploys a project group.
DeployCommand_Remarks: This is the primary command for interacting with project groups. The 'deploy' command first builds all component projects and then executes all targets defined in the project group file. A project group is defined using the <ProjectGroup> tag inside of a compiler configuration file ({0}).
DeployCommand_IgnoreMissingOption: Ignore missing targets and resume deployment.
DeployCommand_FailFastOption: Cancel deployment immediately if any target fails.
DeployCommand_OptionsOption: Additional options passed to the compiler for each project being built.
DeployCommand_Example1: Builds and deploys the project group defined in the current directory.
DeployCommand_Example2: Builds and deploys the project group, ignoring any missing targets.
DeployCommand_Example3: Builds and deploys the project group, stopping immediately if any target fails.
DeployCommand_Example4: Builds and deploys the project group, passing the '-l' flag to the compiler for each project being built.
DeployCommand_NoConfigurationFileInCurre: Current directory contains no configuration file ({0}).
DeployCommand_ConfigurationFileDefinesNo: Invalid configuration file: '{0}' does not define a project group.

DeployCommand_DeploymentTargetNotFound: The deployment target '{0}' could not be found.
DeployCommand_DeploymentTargetNonzeroExi: Deployment target '{0}' ended with a nonzero exit code.
HelpCommand_Description: Lists all available commands and shows help for specific commands or compiler features.
HelpCommand_HelpDetailsDescription: Shows a list of available commands or advanced information about a specific command or topic.
HelpCommand_CommandOption: The name of a compiler command to show help for.
HelpCommand_OptionsOption: Shows a list of all available project file properties.
HelpCommand_SimpleOption: Shows a simplified selection of commands suitable for minimalist developers.
HelpCommand_CommandsOption: Prints a comma-separated list of available commands.
HelpCommand_NoExternalOption: Does not display commands defined by external packages.
HelpCommand_Example1: Displays a list of all available commands.
HelpCommand_Example2: Displays a list of all commands, excluding ones from external packages.
HelpCommand_Example3: Displays a list of all available project file properties.
HelpCommand_Example4: Displays detailed help for the 'build' command.
HelpCommand_CommandNotFound: Could not load help details for '{0}': Command not found.
HelpCommand_IncreaseConsoleWidth: Increase the console width for a better viewing experience.
HelpCommand_DCCompileMoreInformation: Use 'dc help compile' for more information.
HelpCommand_ExecutesCommandFromList: Executes a command from the list below.
HelpCommand_DisplayInformationAboutComma: Displays more information about a specific command.
HelpCommand_NoCommandsAvailable: No commands are available. If the issue persists, try installing an appropriate package or reinstalling the application.
HelpCommand_CommandNoHelpDetails: The command '{0}' does not specify any help details.
NewCommand_Description: Creates the file structure of a Dassie project.
NewCommand_Remarks: This command creates a new directory with the specified project name and creates the file structure of the specified project template inside. Project templates are installed as part of compiler extensions (packages). Managing compiler extensions is facilitated through the 'dc package' command. The Dassie compiler includes the templates 'Console' and 'Library' by default.
NewCommand_TemplateOption: The template to use for the new project.
NewCommand_ForceOption: If enabled, directories colliding with the path of the new project will be deleted.
NewCommand_Example1: Creates a new console application project named 'MyApp' in the current directory.
NewCommand_Example2: Creates a new project based on the template 'custom-template' named 'MyProject' in the current directory. If a directory named 'MyProject' already exists, it will be deleted first.
NewCommand_Example3: Lists all installed project templates along with the package they are installed from.
PackageCommand_ListDescription: Displays a list of all installed extensions.
PackageCommand_InfoDescription: Displays advanced information about the specified extension.
PackageCommand_InstallDescription: Installs the specified extension from the package repository. Use the -g flag to install the package as a globally accessible tool.
PackageCommand_ImportDescription: Installs an extension from the specified file path. Use the -o flag to overwrite existing extensions.
PackageCommand_UpdateDescription: Updates the specified extension to the newest version.
PackageCommand_HelpDetailsDescription: Used to install and manage compiler extensions.
PackageCommand_OptionsOption: Additional options to pass to the subcommand.
PackageCommand_Example1: Displays a list of installed extensions.
PackageCommand_Example3: Installs the extension 'MyExtension' from the package repository.
PackageCommand_Example4: Installs an extension from the specified file path.
PackageCommand_SpecifiedExtensionNotFoun: The specified extension could not be found.
PackageCommand_ExtensionNotFound: The extension '{0}' could not be found.
PackageCommand_ExtensionAlreadyInstalled: The extension '{0}' is already installed. Use the 'dc package update' command to update it to the newest version.
PackageCommand_SpecifiedExtensionFileNot: The specified extension file could not be found.
PackageCommand_ExtensionFileAlreadyInsta: The specified extension is already installed. Use the -o flag to overwrite existing extensions.
PackageCommand_ToolPathNotFound: The specified tool path could not be found.
PackageCommand_BuiltInModuleCannotBeUnin: The built-in module '{0}' cannot be uninstalled.
PackageCommand_ExtensionRemoveWarningLin: Warning: The extension {0} is located inside of an assembly containing multiple extensions. If you proceed with the removal, the following extensions will be removed:
PackageCommand_CommandNotImplemented: This command is not yet implemented.
RunCommand_Description: Compiles a project or project group and then runs the output executable with the specified arguments.
RunCommand_Remarks:
This command requires the presence of a project or project group. If it is executed on a project group, the project that is executed is determined by the <Executable> property in the project group definition.
This command only recompiles the project if the source files have been updated since the last compilation or the output files have been deleted. Otherwise, the executable is launched immediately.
RunCommand_ArgumentsOption: Command-line arguments passed to the program that is executed.
RunCommand_ProfileOption: The build profile to use for compilation. If not specified, the default profile is used.
RunCommand_Example1: Compiles the current project and runs the resulting executable without any arguments.
RunCommand_Example2: Compiles the current project and runs the resulting executable with the arguments 'arg1' and 'arg2'.
RunCommand_Example3: Compiles the project with a specific build profile and runs the resulting executable without any arguments.
RunCommand_ProjectNotExecutable: The current project cannot be executed. Projects with an application type of '{0}' are not executable.
RunCommand_InsufficientInformation: Insufficient information: The files to execute could not be determined. Create a project file (dsconfig.xml) and set the required properties 'BuildDirectory' and 'AssemblyFileName' to enable this command.
ScratchpadCommand_ScratchNotFound: The scratch '{0}' could not be found.
ScratchpadCommand_LoadNotSupportedDefaul: The command 'load' is not supported if the editor is set to 'default'. Use 'dc config --global core.scratchpad.editor=<Editor>' to set a different editor.
ScratchpadCommand_MarkEndOfInput: To mark the end of the input, press Ctrl+Z in an empty line and hit Enter.
ScratchpadCommand_Description: Allows compiling and running Dassie source code from standard input.
ScratchpadCommand_NameOption: Specifies the name of the scratch.
ScratchpadCommand_ConfigOption: The compiler configuration ({0}) file to use.
ScratchpadCommand_Remarks: The 'scratchpad' command reads source code from standard input, compiles it into a temporary executable and immediately executes it. If standard input has been redirected (e.g. through a pipe) and the compilation is successful, the command will produce no output other than the output from the executed program. If no command is specified, the command 'new' is used implicitly.
ScratchpadCommand_OptionsOption: Additional options passed to the subcommand.
ScratchpadCommand_Example1: Starts a new scratchpad session, allowing the user to enter Dassie source code from standard input.
ScratchpadCommand_Example2: Starts a new scratchpad session named 'myScratch'.
TestCommand_Description: Runs unit tests defined for the current project or project group.
TestCommand_Remarks:
If ran on a project, this command first compiles the project and then collects and runs all unit tests defined in the project or specified module. If ran on a project group, the command collects and runs all unit tests from all projects in the group.
A 'test module' is a module decorated with the <TestModule> attribute from the Dassie unit test library (Dassie.Tests). A test is a method of a test module decorated with the <Test> attribute.
TestCommand_AssemblyOption: Run tests from the specified assembly.
TestCommand_ModuleOption: Run tests from the specified test module. Multiple modules can be specified by using the option multiple times.
TestCommand_Example1: Compiles the current project and runs all unit tests defined in it.
TestCommand_Example2: Compiles the current project and runs all unit tests defined in it, only displaying failed tests.
TestCommand_Example3: Compiles the current project and runs all unit tests defined in the test module 'MyNamespace.MyTestModule'.
TestCommand_Example4: Runs all unit tests defined in the specified assembly.
TestCommand_AssemblyNotFound: The assembly '{0}' could not be found.
TestCommand_NotSupportedForProjectGroup: The command 'dc test' is not yet supported for project groups.
TestCommand_AssemblyNotFoundProjectFile: Assembly '{0}' could not be found. Consider setting the <AssemblyFileName> property in the project file.
TestCommand_TestModuleNotFound: The assembly '{0}' contains no test module named '{1}'.
TestCommand_TypeNotValidTestModuleMissin: The type '{0}' in assembly '{1}' is not a valid test module, since it is not marked with the '<TestModule>' attribute.
TestCommand_TypeNotValidTestModuleNotMod: '{0}' from assembly '{1}' cannot be used as a test module since it is not a module. Test modules must not be instantiable.
TestCommand_AssemblyContainsNoTestModule: The assembly '{0}' contains no test modules.
StringHelper_LanguageNotFound: The localization resources for language '{0}' could not be found. The fallback language (en-US) will be used.
WatchCommand_Description: Watches all .ds files in the current folder structure and automatically recompiles when files are changed.
WatchCommand_CommandOptionDescription: Specifies the compiler command that is executed when files are changed. The default value is 'build'.
WatchCommand_ProfileOptionDescription: Specifies the build profile that is used when files are changed. If this option is set, the '--command' option cannot be used.
WatchCommand_DirectoryOptionDescription: Specifies the directory that is watched for changed source files. Cannot be combined with the '--command' and '--profile' options.
WatchCommand_Example2: Re-run the application automatically on save.
WatchCommand_Example3: Watch and rebuild using the Release profile.

WatchCommand_TargetOnlyWithoutOptions: 'dc watch': Compilation target can only be manually specified if neither '--profile' nor '--command' are set.
